title: primula viscosa subsp. pedemontana
intro: Primula viscosa subsp. pedemontana is a subspecies of the Primula viscosa plant, commonly known as sticky primrose or viscid primrose, which is a type of flowering plant in the primrose family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its antitussive, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Primula viscosa subsp. pedemontana include relieving coughs, colds, and flu symptoms, as well as soothing digestive issues such as diarrhea and indigestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
